title: thuja occidentalis f. alba
intro: Thuja occidentalis f. alba, also known as the white arborvitae, is a variant of the Eastern White Cedar, a slow-growing evergreen coniferous tree native to eastern North America. The medicinal properties of Thuja occidentalis f. alba lie in its essential oils, which contain thujone, a compound with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of the common cold. Thuja occidentalis f. alba can be prepared as a herbal tea, infused oil, or tincture, and is often used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
